finding: `broker register` trusts the intent-file payload over CLI `--task` and `--actor`, so a mismatched or cross-contaminated intent file can silently register another task's write intent under the wrong operator flow.
expectedBehavior: `broker register` should fail closed when CLI flags disagree with the payload, and the error should identify the mismatched fields before any registry mutation occurs.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by hardening `packages/cli/src/commands/broker.ts`: `broker register` now enforces payload/flag parity before arbitration or registry writes, and throws `ATM_BROKER_REGISTER_PAYLOAD_FLAG_MISMATCH` with per-field mismatch details when `--task` and/or `--actor` disagree with the intent file. A new focused regression in `scripts/validate-broker-register.ts` proves mismatched task/actor pairs fail closed without mutating `.atm/runtime/write-broker.registry.json`, while matching payloads still register cleanly. The command surface in `packages/cli/src/commands/command-specs/broker.spec.ts` now also advertises `--actor` as a register parity input, not only a runtime-activate input.
followUp: Keep the payload/flag parity regression in `scripts/validate-broker-register.ts`; future broker register changes must preserve fail-closed behavior before any registry mutation when CLI authority and payload disagree.
